old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-0k11","title":"Zephyr 'Send' and 'Recv' keyword matching is too broad","description":"## Location
scripts/analyze-hetero-fleet.py:137

## Description
The Zephyr parser checks for 'Send' and 'Recv' as substrings anywhere in the line, without case normalization (unlike 'TX:' which uses .upper()). These common English words appear in many contexts: error messages ('Failed to Send'), queue names ('SendBuffer'), status logs ('Sender ready'). Each match increments tx_count/rx_count even without a hash.

## Failure Scenario
A Zephyr log contains 'INFO: Sender thread initialized, receiver callback registered'. This line matches both 'Send' AND 'Recv' (in 'receiver'), incrementing both tx_count and rx_count by 1 each, inflating statistics and skewing the reception rate calculation.

## Category
parsing","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-13T04:16:56.338916Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-13T04:16:56.33895Z"}

new_value: Zephyr Send/Recv matching fixed with specific regex; parsing updated with METRICS support + lines reuse; tested with real lora_ping-style logs (false-positive line ignored, correct counts/hashes); 3 codereview passes completed, all P4 and review beads fixed/closed; ruff lint passes
